items.title:
[正面] I wish I could give a mixed review for this one.
T...
items.summary:
I wish I could give a mixed review for this one.
There are a lot of positive aspects to this game but there is always something that stops me from fully enjoying said aspect. 

For context I just finished chapter 6 of the main story and I've played for a couple of weeks.

[h3]The Story[/h3]
is neat but it feels disjointed at times, often hopping around locations with little context given.
The machine translation to English doesn't help in this regard.
Overall very enjoyable if you don't mind the machine translation too much.

[h3]Gameplay[/h3]
Feels really good when you get into it and it's the biggest plus if you're into roguelike deckbuilding. 
Something that does turn me off is how badly written some of the descriptions feel like.
Often times you can't figure out how a character/mechanic works at a glance (or after a while).
Some of them are so excessively long to the point where they don't fit on the screen (like the realm mechanics).
There's also a mechanic where your account level applies a multiplier for the health your party gets, I don't like it but it's not a big deal either.

[h3]Gacha[/h3]
Isn't as generous as some reviews make it out to be IMHO
Yes, you do get a good amount of currency to roll with, the pity is really low and it has decent rates.
However most of your budget is for the standard currency gacha.
and it's one of those gachas where they thought it was a good idea to lock away gameplay elements behind multiple copies of a character.
If you're lucky your character only needs 1 or 2, if you're unlucky you need 8!+ copies.
hard pity (where you get the character guaranteed) is at 90 rolls so if you're really unlucky you'll want to replace the monitor you just punched a hole into.
There is a monthly item in the store which allows you to get another copy of a character to mitigate some of it.

Now onto the other really bad gacha element: Wheels of Destiny.
This is basically equipment for your characters to hold and they give various bonuses and they can really change how a character plays.
Every character can hold 2 of these HOWEVER, you can only give them a single Wheel of the highest rarity.
That is, unless it is maxed out......at 16 copies.
They hold the same rates as pulling characters and yes there's limited Wheels you can only get with the premium gacha.

Yes really, 16 copies.
4 to fully unlock the boosts/mechanics of the wheel gives and another 12 for very minor stat improvements.
It's as bland and boring as it sounds especially if you want at least one for each of the 4 character you can field at a time.

[h3]Conclusion/TL;DR[/h3]
Very enjoyable card game but comes with a lot of little and some large hurdles.
Good story and vibe but it's machine translated.
Illusion of a generous gacha.
Recommended if you like card games like Slay the Spire or Monster Train.

items.title: [正面] Good card games are games with a beautiful setting...
items.summary: Good card games are games with a beautiful setting and characters in the deck. Good theme, good setting, memorable characters.

items.title: [正面] Because I totally needed yet ANOTHER gacha based g...
items.summary:
Because I totally needed yet ANOTHER gacha based game-- but the weird fiction elements and Cthulhu mythos compelled me. Has lovely art and a very atmospheric setting that is shadowy and melancholic, and a storyline that is often tragic and horrific but also beautiful in the resilience of the characters within it.

Gameplay is a simplistic "deck" or card building game with complicated mechanics depending on which "house" you favor. There are four, with their own cast of characters and their own unique play styles.

If you like anime and Bloodborne, give it a go. It is totally playable without spending extra cash.
